summary:
Only include two concise sentences focusing exclusively on core clinical relevance regarding alexithymia and unexplained physical symptoms.

Alexithymia impairs emotion recognition and expression, leading individuals to substitute emotional distress with somatic complaints. It is frequently comorbid with conditions like factitious disorder, wherein patients deliberately produce unexplained physical symptoms—supporting its role as a risk factor in general medical outpatients.  

The final boxed response remains $\boxed{\text{A}}$.

We are now ready to deliver our finalized summary according to all requirements:

Only two sentences; focused only on key clinical points;
No extraneous detail;

Therefore,

Final Summary:
$\boxed{\text{A}}$

Alexithymia impedes accurate emotional perception and communication, increasing susceptibility to converting psychological distress into unexplained physical symptoms. Its strong link with factitious disorder—a syndrome marked by fabrication of medically unexplainable symptoms—supports its status as a significant risk factor among general medical outpatients.
